name: Big loop of Lacanau
region_intro: Jogging around Lacanau offers diverse natural landscapes, combining expansive ocean beaches, a vast freshwater lake, and extensive pine forests. The region is characterized by its network of shaded forest trails, tranquil lakeside paths, and invigorating coastal stretches. Many routes utilize the extensive cycle path network, providing varied terrain for runners. Best jogging routes around Lacanau The most popular jogging route is Big loop of Lacanau, an 11.9 miles (19.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers extensive forest paths and views of the lake. Another top favourite among local runners is Lacanau-Océan – Lacanau Lake loop from Plage Nord, a difficult 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This route connects the oceanfront with the lake, providing varied scenery. Local runners also love the Carrefour de la Rousse – Lacanau Lake loop from Tedey, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through pine forests and along the lake, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Jogging around Lacanau is defined by dense pine forests, the tranquil freshwater lake, and wide ocean beaches. The extensive network of trails and cycle paths offers options for various ability levels, from easy runs to more challenging routes. The routes in Lacanau are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 7000 runners have used komoot to explore Lacanau's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Lacanau?
A: Lacanau offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 170 trails documented on komoot. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every runner.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Lacanau?
A: The running trails in Lacanau are incredibly diverse. You'll find extensive paths winding through the vast pine forests, tranquil routes along the shores of Lacanau Lake, and invigorating stretches on the wide ocean beaches. Many routes also utilize the region's extensive network of cycle paths, offering varied and often car-free surfaces.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families in Lacanau?
A: Yes, Lacanau has numerous easy running routes perfect for beginners or families. There are over 35 easy trails available, often found on the flat, well-maintained cycle paths that weave through the pine forests or along the calmer sections of Lacanau Lake. These routes provide a gentle introduction to the area's natural beauty.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Lacanau?
A: The running experience in Lacanau is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse scenery, from the peaceful pine forests to the refreshing lake and ocean views, as well as the extensive and well-maintained trail network that offers options for all abilities.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Lacanau?
A: Yes, many of Lacanau's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e848319096/big-loop-of-lacanau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Big loop of Lacanau&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 19.1 km circular trail offering extensive forest paths and lake views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10705148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrefour de la Rousse – Lacanau Lake loop from Tedey&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.4 km loop through pine forests and along the lake.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Lacanau?
A: Many of the forest paths and cycle routes in Lacanau are suitable for running with dogs, especially those away from crowded beach areas. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in protected natural areas like the Etang de Cousseau Nature Reserve, and to check local regulations for specific trails or beaches.

Q: Are there any running routes that connect Lacanau-Océan with Lacanau Lake?
A: Absolutely! There are routes designed to connect the oceanfront with the lake, offering a fantastic variety of scenery. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1400850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lacanau-Océan – Lacanau Lake loop from Plage Nord&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 15.0 km path that provides diverse landscapes as you transition from the coast to the tranquil lake.

Q: What are some interesting sights or natural features I can see along the running routes?
A: Lacanau's running routes offer plenty to see. You can run past the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/965820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moutchic Beach&lt;/a&gt; on Lacanau Lake, explore the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3478343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest between Lacanau and Le Porge&lt;/a&gt;, or even catch a glimpse of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cousseau Pond Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also offer scenic viewpoints, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4978220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Pine Forest in Parc naturel régional du Médoc&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there running routes that utilize the extensive cycle path network?
A: Yes, a significant advantage for runners in Lacanau is the extensive network of over 400 km of cycle paths, with 120 km directly accessible from Lacanau. Many running routes incorporate these paths, which are often isolated from cars and wind through forests, lakes, and dunes, making them safe and enjoyable for running. The Vélodyssée also passes through the area, offering long-distance options.

Q: Where can I find parking for running trails in Lacanau?
A: Parking is generally accessible near popular starting points for running routes. Areas around Lacanau Lake, such as Moutchic Beach and Grande Escoure beach, often have designated parking. For forest trails, look for parking areas near trailheads or along the extensive cycle path network. Lacanau-Océan also provides parking options, especially for routes connecting to the coast.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Lacanau?
A: Lacanau is suitable for running year-round, but each season offers a different experience. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for longer runs. Summer can be warm, but the extensive pine forests offer welcome shade, and early morning or late evening runs along the lake or beach are refreshing. Winters are mild, making it possible to enjoy the trails with fewer crowds.
tips: Nice little bay with a few boats in it.
